description: Frozen trace-scorecard v1 contract for the agent-delivery-harness (issue #104). Machine-readable authority for the cross-run scorecard emitted by scripts/trace-scorecard.sh to <main root>/tests/evals/scorecards/trace-scorecard.json (single stable filename, idempotent whole-file overwrite, never append, no generation timestamp — reruns over unchanged inputs are byte-identical). Consumes the per-issue trace-summary v1 objects (trace-summary.v1.json) plus one sanctioned peek at a sibling trace.jsonl for version attribution. Sibling of trace-summary.v1.json, same documentation style.

notes.absence_semantics: null means no data; 0 means measured zero; [] means the detector/collector ran and found nothing. A bucket where no run carried token data emits tokens null (never a fabricated {input: 0, output: 0}); token_coverage {runs_with_tokens, of} carries the honest denominator. Rates always carry an explicit of/coverage denominator so absent data can never dilute a rate.
notes.open_world: Additive top-level keys are allowed in later v1.x revisions; existing keys and their semantics are frozen. Consumers reject unknown majors of scorecard_schema_version. The same rule is applied upstream: an input summary with an unknown major of summary_schema_version is skipped untouched (listed under inputs.skipped), never interpreted under the trace-summary v1 contract.
notes.single_source_of_numbers: The markdown report on stdout is rendered FROM this object, so the human report and this machine scorecard can never disagree.
notes.computed_only: Every value is computed from the trace-summary v1 objects on disk (plus the sanctioned attribution peek at trace.jsonl); nothing is self-reported and nothing is regenerated or repaired. Missing summaries are reported under inputs.missing_summaries with the trace-report.sh regeneration hint; unreadable or unknown-major summaries under inputs.skipped.
notes.version_attribution: A run whose summary carries exactly one harness_versions element is attributed to it (attribution "single"). A multi-version run with a readable sibling trace.jsonl is attributed to the LAST version-carrying span's harness.version (attribution "last_seen_in_trace") — never the summary's sort order, which is meaningless for git SHAs, and never simply the last line. Unattributable runs are never guessed: a multi-version run without a readable trace, or a run that recorded no harness.version at all (harness_versions []), lands in the visible synthetic "mixed" bucket (attribution "unresolved_mixed") with the full harness_versions list preserved on the run row. A summary whose harness_versions is not an array is malformed and is skipped-with-note (inputs.skipped), never routed into attribution.
notes.deferred_metrics: Honest computable-vs-deferred split: review-blocking findings per issue are DEFERRED (trace-summary v1 carries no per-verdict field; per-stage handback step counts are only a labeled proxy) and per-feature tool-call attribution is DEFERRED (v1 tools[] has no feature dimension). Both need an additive trace-summary v1.x extension before this scorecard can carry them — they are never fabricated from what v1 carries.
notes.issue_62_mapping: Mapping to #62 (L0/L1 local eval runner) is documented, not forked: by_version[].issues[] rows are RUN AGGREGATES over real issue runs, not graded eval cases — a case-level eval scorecard (manifest/fixture/label/prediction rows) is a different artifact, and reusing its row shape here would force fake label/prediction fields. This scorecard shares #62's top-level conventions where they genuinely apply (a schema-version field, runtime, a rows+aggregates split). If/when #62 lands, its runner can either adopt this run-row shape for trace aggregation or map each by_version[].issues[] row into its own results[] with boundary "trace-aggregate". The version field is the seam: consumers reject unknown majors; additive keys are allowed in 1.x.
notes.visibility: Local-only, never committed; covered by the .gitignore rule pair tests/evals/scorecards/* + !tests/evals/scorecards/.gitkeep (generated scorecards are local artifacts per the l0 eval spec).

fields.runtime: string; "local" for this generator (mirrors the #62 runtime convention).
fields.source_root: string; the <main root>/.copilot-tracking/issues directory the summaries were scanned from.
fields.summary_schema_versions_seen: array of unique numbers; every summary_schema_version seen on the AGGREGATED summaries (skipped files contribute nothing).
fields.inputs: object {summaries_found, missing_summaries, skipped}. summaries_found counts AGGREGATED summaries only. missing_summaries lists dirs with a trace.jsonl but no trace-summary.json as {issue_dir, trace_file, hint} — hint is the trace-report.sh regeneration command (reported, never repaired). skipped lists summaries excluded from every aggregate as {summary_file, reason} (malformed JSON, or an unknown major of summary_schema_version).
fields.by_version: array; one bucket per attributed harness version (plus the synthetic "mixed" bucket when unresolved multi-version runs exist), sorted by version key. Every aggregated run lands in exactly one bucket.
fields.by_version[].harness_version: string; the attributed harness.version, or "mixed" for unattributable runs (multi-version with no readable trace to peek, or no harness.version recorded at all).
fields.by_version[].runs: number; runs aggregated into the bucket.
fields.by_version[].red_reentry_free_rate: object {free, of}; free counts finished, passing runs whose red_reentry is [] — this measures no-red-after-green re-entry, NOT literally first-pass green (a red before a feature's first green is invisible to trace-summary v1); of is the bucket's run count (explicit denominator).
fields.by_version[].deviations: object {count, feature_ids}; count sums deviations.count across the bucket's runs (measured zero is a real 0); feature_ids is the unique union.
fields.by_version[].tool_calls: object {calls, fail_calls}; sums over the runs' tools[] entries.
fields.by_version[].tokens: object {input, output} summed over ONLY the runs whose summary carries a non-null tokens object, or null when NO run in the bucket carried token data (absence is null, never 0; a tokens-null run is never averaged in as 0). Input token buckets are accepted under either key pair — {input, output} or the trace-report.sh producer's {input_tokens, output_tokens} — and always emitted here as {input, output}.
fields.by_version[].token_coverage: object {runs_with_tokens, of}; the honest denominator for the tokens sums.
fields.by_version[].tool_coverage: object {runs_with_tool_spans, of}; the honest denominator for tool_calls, mirroring token_coverage (issue #131). runs_with_tool_spans counts runs whose summary coverage.has_tool_spans is true; of is the bucket run count. A lifecycle-only run (no runtime adapter, no tool spans) is counted in of but not in runs_with_tool_spans, so a low tool_calls sum reads as 'the adapter was not wired' rather than 'the agent called no tools' — instrumented and lifecycle-only runs are never blended. Additive open-world optional key; absent on scorecards generated before #131.
fields.by_version[].issues: array of per-run rows, each {issue, summary_file, attribution, harness_versions, finished, final_outcome, red_reentry, deviations, tool_calls, tool_fail_calls, wall_clock_elapsed_seconds, tokens, loop_indicator_groups, invalid_lines}. summary_file is generated-from provenance; attribution is one of "single", "last_seen_in_trace", "unresolved_mixed"; harness_versions preserves the summary's full version list; wall_clock_elapsed_seconds and tokens propagate null as null; coverage propagates the summary's coverage object (has_tool_spans/has_model_spans) or null for a pre-#131 summary; invalid_lines surfaces the summary's span_counts.invalid_lines (a scorecard that hides bad input is not honest).
